Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Understand the Goal The user wants a detailed explanation of a business process represented in pseudo BPMN format The explanation needs to cover the purpose of each step gateway meanings path convergence looping differences between standard and custom requests and conditions for approvals or rework

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Analyze the Pseudo BPMN Start Receive Customer Request Gateway 1 XOR Exclusive Check Request Type Standard vs Custom Standard Path Task B1 Perform Standard Validation Gateway 2 AND Parallel Run Parallel Checks Task C1 Credit Check and Task C2 Inventory Check run simultaneously Join wait for both to finish Task D Calculate Delivery Date Custom Path Task B2 Perform Custom Feasibility Analysis Gateway 3 XOR Is Customization Feasible Yes No Yes to Task E1 Prepare Custom Quotation No to Task E2 Send Rejection Notice then End Event Convergence After Standard Task D or Custom Task E1 Path Tasks Completed Gateway 4 XOR Is Approval Needed Yes No Approval Needed Path Task F Obtain Manager Approval Gateway 5 XOR Is Approval Granted Yes No Yes to Task G Generate Final Invoice No to Task H Re evaluate Conditions Loop back to Task E1 Custom or Task D Standard No Approval Needed Path Task G Generate Final Invoice End Phase Task I Send Confirmation to Customer End Event

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Structure the Response Introduction High level summary of the business process handling customer requests distinguishing between standard and custom orders Process Breakdown by Phase Phase 1 Intake and Routing Request Type Phase 2 Execution of Standard vs Custom Paths Highlight differences Phase 3 Approval Workflow Gateways approvals and loops Phase 4 Finalization Detailed Explanation of Gateways Explicitly define XOR and AND gateways used Conditions for Approvals and Rework Summarize when these are triggered Conclusion Brief wrap up

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Drafting the Content Intro The process models a customer order request fulfillment system It handles standard and custom requests differently but unifies them for final approval invoicing and confirmation Phase 1 Intake Task A captures the customer request The first XOR gateway Check Request Type acts as a router If standard go one way if custom go another XOR means mutually exclusive paths Phase 2 Standard vs Custom Differences Standard Task B1 validates the request Because standard orders usually involve multiple independent verifications an AND gateway splits the flow into parallel tasks C1 Credit Check and C2 Inventory Check They run concurrently to save time Once both are done AND join Task D calculates the delivery date based on available inventory and credit terms Custom Task B2 conducts a feasibility analysis An XOR gateway checks feasibility If No Task E2 sends a rejection notice and the process ends early If Yes Task E1 prepares a custom quotation Phase 3 Convergence and Approval The paths converge If the request went through Standard Task D or Custom Task E1 it hits an XOR gateway Is Approval Needed If No skip straight to Task G Generate Final Invoice If Yes Task F obtains manager approval Another XOR gateway checks the managers decision If Granted go to Task G Generate Final Invoice If Not Granted Task H Re evaluate Conditions triggers a loop The re evaluation sends the process back depending on the original path to Task E1 Custom to revise the quote or Task D Standard to recalculate the delivery date Phase 4 Finalization After Task G Invoice generated Task I sends the confirmation to the customer leading to the end of the process unless it was rejected in Phase 2 Gateway Summary XOR Exclusive Used for decision making where only one path can be taken Request Type Feasibility Approval Needed Approval Granted AND Parallel Used to split tasks to run simultaneously and join them when all are complete Credit and Inventory checks Approvals Rework Summary Approval is needed when business rules dictate it for example high value or specific custom terms Rework Task H happens if a manager denies approval requiring adjustments to either the standard delivery parameters or the custom quotation before resubmitting for approval

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Self correction The pseudo BPMN implies a conditional loop based on the original path Mention this explicitly

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Review and Refine Ensure all constraints of the prompt are met Purpose of steps Yes Gateway meanings Yes Convergence looping Yes Standard vs custom differences Yes Approval rework conditions Yes Tone should be professional and analytical Formatting with bolding and bullet points will make it readable

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - IND
Text: Final Polish Produce the final output based on the structured thoughts
